ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
Subject id assigned by the NRGR biorepository.
Study number assigned by NIMH when the study is registered with NRGR.
1= hiPSC; 2= Fibroblast; 3= Other (other_cell_type)
Use a Cell Line Ontology or NCI Thesaurus term if possible.
Identifier assigned by NRGR to the study responsible for submitting this cell line. NOTE: this value may be identical to nrgr_study_id and/or source_study_id
Unique identifier of source cell line from which the current cell line is derived. Use of a biobank identifier is strongly preferred.
1= Biobank ID; 2= Study- or submitter-designated ID; 3= Other type to be described in other_source_cell_id_type

ElementDescription: Source cell line ID type, other
Notes: Corresponding database or repository from which the cell line was sourced. For example: ''sampled'',''coriell'',''cellosaurus'', ''clo'', ''ebisc'', etc.

ElementDescription: Source cell type from which the current cell line was derived
Notes: 1=Adipose tissue derived mesenchymal stem cell; 2=Amniocyte; 3=Derived peripheral blood mononuclear cell; 4=Erythroblast; 5=Fibroblast; 6=Fibroblast of dermis; 7=Foreskin fibroblast; 8=hiPSC; 9=Keratinocyte; 10=Mononuclear cell; 11=Peripheral blood mononuclear cell; 12=Skin fibroblast; 13=Urine; 14=Other (other_source_cell_type)

ElementDescription: Source cell type from which the current cell line was derived, other
Notes: Use a term listed in the EMBL-EBI Ontology Lookup Service if possible.
Identifier assigned by NRGR to the study that generated the source cell line from which the current cell line is derived. NOTE: this value may be identical to nrgr_study_id and/or submitter_study_id.

ElementDescription: Allen Cell Collection cell line ID
Notes: Allen Cell Collection ID (https://www.allencell.org/cell-catalog.html) if available.
BioSamples ID (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/biosamples/) if available.
Cellosaurus ID (https://web.expasy.org/cellosaurus/) if available.

ElementDescription: Cell Line Ontology cell line ID
Notes: Cell Line Ontology ID (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/clo) if available.

ElementDescription: European Bank for Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells cell line ID
Notes: European Bank For Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (EBiSC) ID (https://ebisc.org/) if available.

ElementDescription: European Collection of Authenticated Cell Cultures cell line ID
Notes: European Collection of Authenticated Cell Cultures (ECACC) ID (https://www.phe-culturecollections.org.uk/collections/ecacc.aspx) if available.

ElementDescription: Human Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell Initiative cell line ID
Notes: Human Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell Initiative ID (https://www.hipsci.org/) if available.

ElementDescription: Human Pluripotent Stem Cell Registry cell line ID
Notes: Human Pluripotent Stem Cell Registry ID (https://hpscreg.eu/) if available.

ElementDescription: NIH Human Embryonic Stem Cell Registry cell line ID
Notes: NIH Human Embryonic Stem Cell Registry ID (https://grants.nih.gov/stem_cells/registry/current.htm) if available.
Name of other database where this cell line is registered.
ID of cell line in other database.

ElementDescription: Subject has known genetic dx (e.g., Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, Autism, etc.)
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
Use a term listed in the EMBL-EBI Ontology Lookup Service if possible.

ElementDescription: Genetic dx ascertained by physical exam
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: Genetic dx ascertained by medical history
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: Genetic dx cytogenetic test method used
Genetic dx ascertained by biological assay
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: Genetic dx biological assay method used

Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: Genetic dx ascertained by other method
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: Genetic dx ascertained by other method, description
Genetic dx has known causal variants
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
Name of gene targeted by first variant (use a HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee term) or chromosomal region targeted by first variant (use cytogenetic band nomenclature).
Cytogenetic band position of first variant. Use International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN). E.g., 12q12 or 19q13.3.

Nucleotide sequence of first variant in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: NM_198578.4(LRRK2):c.7153G>A or NM_000138.4:c4082G>A. Notice that the reference sequence and variant codes are mandatory, but the gene name is optional.

Protein sequence of first variant in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: XP_005268686.1:p.Gly2385Arg. Note that the reference sequence and variant codes are both mandatory.

ElementDescription: Genetic dx variant 1 homogeneous or heterogeneous
Notes: 1= Homozygous; 2= Heterozygous

ElementDescription: Genetic dx variant 1 dbSNP RefSNP number

Notes: Name of gene targeted by second variant (use a HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee term) or chromosomal region targeted by second variant (use cytogenetic band nomenclature).
Cytogenetic band position of second variant. Use International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN). E.g., 12q12 or 19q13.3.

Nucleotide sequence of second variant in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: NM_198578.4(LRRK2):c.7153G>A or NM_000138.4:c4082G>A. Notice that the reference sequence and variant codes are mandatory, but the gene name is optional.

Protein sequence of second variant in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: XP_005268686.1:p.Gly2385Arg. Note that the reference sequence and variant codes are both mandatory.

ElementDescription: Genetic dx variant 2 homogeneous or heterogeneous
Notes: 1= Homozygous; 2= Heterozygous

ElementDescription: Genetic dx variant 2 dbSNP RefSNP number
Subject has another known genetic dx
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
Use a term listed in the EMBL-EBI Ontology Lookup Service if possible.
Describe known cytogenetic abnormalities using International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature.
0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: Has expected karyotype (consistent with expected genetic state of the cell line)
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
Passage number at which karyotyping was performed.

ElementDescription: Karyotype of cell using International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature
Cell line has induced genetic modification 1
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
Use a HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee term.
1= Gene knock-in; 2= Gene knock-out; 3= Isogenic modification; 4= Transgene expression; 5= Variant; 6= Other (other_gen_mod_1_type)
Name of transgene (or reporter) introduced by first genetic modification.
Effect or resistance manifested by transgene (e.g., green fluorescence or puromycin resistance).
Cytogenetic band position of first genetic modification. Use International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN). E.g., 12q12 or 19q13.3.

Nucleotide sequence of first genetic modification in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: NM_198578.4(LRRK2):c.7153G>A or NM_000138.4:c4082G>A. Notice that the reference sequence and variant codes are mandatory, but the gene name is optional.

Protein sequence of first genetic modification in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: XP_005268686.1:p.Gly2385Arg. Note that the reference sequence and variant codes are both mandatory.
1= Homozygous; 2= Heterozygous

ElementDescription: Genetic modification 1 effect on target locus
Notes: 1= Changed; 2= Mutated; 3= Repaired

ElementDescription: Genetic modification 1 delivery method (How was the first genetic modification introduced?)
Notes: 1= CRISPR-associated (CRISPR/Cas) System; 2= Homologous Recombination; 3= TALEN; 4= Viral; 5= Zinc finger nuclease; 6= Lentivirus; 7= Other (other_gen_1_delivery_method)

ElementDescription: Cell line has induced genetic modification 2
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes
Use a HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee term.
1= Gene knock-in; 2= Gene knock-out; 3= Isogenic modification; 4= Transgene expression; 5= Variant; 6= Other (other_gen_mod_2_type)
Name of transgene (or reporter) introduced by second genetic modification.
Effect or resistance manifested by transgene (e.g., green fluorescence or puromycin resistance).
Cytogenetic band position of second genetic modification. Use International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN). E.g., 12q12 or 19q13.3.

Nucleotide sequence of second genetic modification in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: NM_198578.4(LRRK2):c.7153G>A or NM_000138.4:c4082G>A. Notice that the reference sequence and variant codes are mandatory, but the gene name is optional.

Protein sequence of second genetic modification in Sequence Variant Nomenclature as defined by the Human Genome Variation Society. For example: XP_005268686.1:p.Gly2385Arg. Note that the reference sequence and variant codes are both mandatory.
1= Homozygous; 2= Heterozygous

ElementDescription: Genetic modification 2 effect on target locus
Notes: 1= Changed; 2= Mutated; 3= Repaired

ElementDescription: Genetic modification 2 delivery method (How was the second genetic modification introduced?)
Notes: 1= CRISPR-associated (CRISPR/Cas) System; 2= Homologous Recombination; 3= TALEN; 4= Viral; 5= Zinc finger nuclease; 6= Lentivirus; 7= Other (other_gen_2_delivery_method)
1= Non-integrating; 2= Integrating
1= Sendai virus; 2= Episomal; 3= Virus (Retrovirus); 4= Virus (Lentivirus); 5= mRNA; 6= Other (other_reprogramming_vector)
Use HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee terms. Separate multiple factors with the pipe ''|'' character. For example: SOX2|KLF4|POU5F1|MYC.
